comments.author: Alexander Terp
comments.body: Investigated and fixed, but the fix differs from the card's diagnosis. Catch-divergence narrowing would have been a no-op (error arms of fallible calls are already stripped at call sites by maybeStripFallible) and unsound for error values (statement-catch fires on panics only, not error values - verified at runtime). The actual cause of the int|dynamic <= int hints from #139: union operands with a dynamic arm went through the operator overload table per-arm, where dynamic has no entries, while bare dynamic operands were deliberately lenient. Fixed by giving dynamic-like arms the same leniency. Verified against the original remind script: the L225 'int|dynamic <= int' hint is gone. The one remaining hint there (L509 'int|dynamic|str - int') has a different cause - map literals widen to { str: V-join } so parsed.due picks up a str arm; filed separately.

creator: Alexander Terp
description: The checker has flow-sensitive narrowing (rts/check/narrow.go) but does not narrow after a catch: block ending in return/exit, so legitimate error-handling code gets int|dynamic <= int hints. Narrowing after terminating catch blocks would remove most of this noise. From #139.
title: rad check: narrow types after catch blocks that return/exit
